examples.title: Bit Stuffing Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to perform bit stuffing on data before transmission
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Bit_Stuffing(input_data, output_stuffed)
BEGIN
 ones_count = 0
 output_stuffed = ""
 
 FOR each bit in input_data DO
 IF bit == '1' THEN
 ones_count = ones_count + 1
 ELSE
 ones_count = 0
 END IF
 
 output_stuffed = output_stuffed + bit
 
 IF ones_count == 5 THEN
 output_stuffed = output_stuffed + '0'
 ones_count = 0
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN output_stuffed
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm scans each bit of the input data. It maintains a counter of consecutive '1' bits. Whenever five consecutive '1' bits are encountered, a '0' is automatically inserted to prevent the flag pattern from appearing in the data.

examples.title: Bit Destuffing Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to remove stuffed bits at the receiver to recover original data
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Bit_Destuffing(input_stuffed, output_data)
BEGIN
 ones_count = 0
 output_data = ""
 
 FOR each bit in input_stuffed DO
 IF bit == '1' THEN
 ones_count = ones_count + 1
 ELSE
 ones_count = 0
 END IF
 
 output_data = output_data + bit
 
 IF ones_count == 5 THEN
 // Skip the next bit (the stuffed '0')
 ones_count = 0
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN output_data
END
examples.explanation: The receiver's destuffing algorithm works similarly but removes the stuffed '0' bit after detecting five consecutive '1' bits. This reverses the stuffing process to recover the original data.

examples.title: CRC-32 Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to compute Frame Check Sequence using CRC-32
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_FCS(data)
BEGIN
 // CRC-32 polynomial: 0x04C11DB7
 polynomial = 0x04C11DB7
 crc = 0xFFFFFFFF
 
 // Pad data with 32 zero bits
 padded_data = data + 32 zeros
 
 FOR each bit in padded_data DO
 // Check if MSB of CRC is 1
 IF (crc & 0x80000000) != 0 THEN
 crc = (crc << 1) XOR polynomial
 ELSE
 crc = crc << 1
 END IF
 
 // XOR with input bit
 crc = crc XOR (bit value << 31)
 END FOR
 
 // Return one's complement
 RETURN NOT crc
END
examples.explanation: CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) uses polynomial division to compute a checksum. The sender computes FCS over the frame and appends it. The receiver performs the same calculation and compares to detect any errors.
